name: PPI-1019
description: Apan (PPI-1019) is developed by Praecis Pharmaceuticals to treat Alzheimer's Disease.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in alzheimer's disease.
mechanismOfAction: PPI-1019 (Apan) inhibits the aggregation of beta-amyloid and its associated nerve cell toxicity. In addition, Apan reaches the brain in quantities that are sufficient to block the aggregation of beta-amyloid molecules and alter the course of the disease. Accumulation of beta-amyloid in the brain is often thought of as a defect in the ability to clear beta-amyloid from the brain through the cerebral spinal fluid (CSF). Both humans and transgenic mice with Alzheimer's disease plaques show increased levels of beta-amyloid in the brain and decreased levels in the CSF. Transgenic mice treated with Apan show significant increases in beta-amyloid levels in the CSF, indicating that Apan is able to mobilize beta-amyloid in the brain and may be facilitating its clearance.
